com.mentor.chs.plugin

Interface IXBaseCurrentContext

  • All Known Subinterfaces:
    IXApplicationContext, IXCurrentContext, IXCurrentSymbolContext, IXSymbolContext

    @NotToBeImplemented
public interface IXBaseCurrentContext
    Interface that provides contextual information to custom actions/plugins that are common to design tools and capitalsymbol. This information may change between invocations.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      IXLanguage getActiveLanguage()
      Gives the X language for the currently active language in the application.
      IXUser getCurrentUser() 
      <O extends IXObject>
      java.util.Set<O>      		 getSelectedObjects(java.lang.Class<O> filterClass)
      Return the set of IXObjects that are currently selected on the diagram plus the selections in the project window but of types filtered by filterClass.

    • Method Detail

      • getSelectedObjects

        <O extends IXObject> java.util.Set<O> getSelectedObjects(java.lang.Class<O> filterClass)

        Return the set of IXObjects that are currently selected on the diagram plus the selections in the project window but of types filtered by filterClass. This method will never return null, but will return an empty set if no objects are selected.

        Parameters:
        filterClass - to filter the objects for the selection set
        Returns:
        an unmodifiable set of selected objects. Returned set will be a typed list.
        Throws:
        java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException - - if an attempt is made to modify the returned set.

        Here is the example of using this 

                Set<@link IXConnectivityObject> xObjs = applicationContext.getSelectedObjects(IXConnectivityObject.class);

      • getActiveLanguage

        IXLanguage getActiveLanguage()
        Gives the X language for the currently active language in the application.
        Returns:
        IXLanguage representing the currently active language in the application.

      • getCurrentUser

        IXUser getCurrentUser()
        Returns:
        The current User information
